title: cardamine enneaphyllos var. alternifolia
intro: Cardamine enneaphyllos var. alternifolia, also known as wood sorrel, is a variant of the enneaphyllous wood sorrel plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Cardamine enneaphyllos var. alternifolia include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and support kidney function, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for a convenient and accessible way to harness its medicinal properties.
